content.problemStatement:
Consider an array BBB of size MMM.

We must perform the following operations with parameter i=M−1,M−2,…,1i = M-1, M-2, \ldots, 1i=M−1,M−2,…,1 (in this order):

Note that the order of operations is different than SUBSEQII.


Choose an index jjj such that i<j≤Mi \lt j \le Mi<j≤M;
Replace BjB_jBj​ in the array with max⁡(0,Bj−Bi)\max(0, B_j - B_i)max(0,Bj​−Bi​).

Note that the subsequent operations will be performed with the updated array BBB.
At the end of all operations, let f(B)f(B)f(B) denote the **maximum possible sum** of all elements of array BBB.

You are given an array AAA of size NNN, you must first find f(A)f(A)f(A), and then handle QQQ updates of the form :


Given integers XXX and YYY, set AX=YA_X = YAX​=Y. Now, print the new value of f(A)f(A)f(A).

Note that the changes made to the array AAA are permanent.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ — the size of the array AAA and the number of queries.
The line contains NNN space-separated integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The next QQQ lines each contain 222 integers, the values of the parameters XXX and YYY for this query.




Output Format
For each test case, output Q+1Q + 1Q+1 lines, the initial value of f(A)f(A)f(A) and the new values of f(A)f(A)f(A) after each query.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of QQQ do not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105 over all test cases.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The initial array is [1,2,4][1, 2, 4][1,2,4].

Pick j=3j = 3j=3 for i=2i = 2i=2. The array becomes [1,2,2][1, 2, 2][1,2,2]
Pick j=3j = 3j=3 for i=1i = 1i=1. The array becomes [1,2,1][1, 2, 1][1,2,1], which has a sum of 444.

It can be shown it's impossible to do better.
After 111st update : the array A=[10,2,4]A = [10, 2, 4]A=[10,2,4] and picking j=3j = 3j=3 for both the operations yields us a final array [10,2,0][10, 2, 0][10,2,0], which has a sum of 121212, which is again provably optimal.
After 555th update : the array A=[3,2,10]A = [3, 2, 10]A=[3,2,10] and picking j=3j = 3j=3 for operation with i=2i = 2i=2, and then j=2j = 2j=2 for operation with i=1i = 1i=1 gives us a final array [3,0,8][3, 0, 8][3,0,8], which gives us a sum of 111111. This is the optimal answer.
Note that choosing j=3j = 3j=3 for both operations would yield a sum of 101010.
